steps.title: Find the growth factor
steps.content:
Let k = b^{1/n}. Then f(x) = a·k^x.
Using the given values: $$\frac{f(8)}{f(5)} = \frac{a k^8}{a k^5} = k^3 = \frac{189}{7} = 27$$
Thus k^3 = 27, so k = 3.

steps.title: Compute f(10)
steps.content:
First find a: $$a = \frac{f(5)}{k^5} = \frac{7}{3^5} = \frac{7}{243}$$
Then $$f(10) = a·k^{10} = \frac{7}{243}·3^{10} = 7·3^5 = 7·243 = 1701$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
